description: Phishing templates impersonating Microsoft products and services

templates.name: Microsoft Account Suspicious Sign-In
suggested_subject_lines: Action required: Review recent sign-in from Bogotá, Colombia
Security alert: Unrecognized device accessed your Microsoft account
templates.notes: Use {{.Date}} for current date realism. The specific location (Bogotá) adds believability. Works across all Microsoft 365 users.

templates.name: SharePoint – Document Shared
suggested_subject_lines: A document has been shared with you
{{.FirstName}}, a file was shared with you on SharePoint
"Q3 Budget" has been shared with you
templates.notes: Shared-document lure is among the highest-clicking Microsoft scenarios. Universal across Microsoft 365 orgs.

templates.name: Microsoft 365 – Password Expiration
suggested_subject_lines: Action required: your password is about to expire
Keep your Microsoft 365 password to avoid sign-out
templates.notes: Password-expiry for the Microsoft category. Pairs with the suspicious-sign-in template for a multi-touch sequence.

templates.name: Microsoft Teams – Missed Messages
suggested_subject_lines: You have 3 missed messages in Microsoft Teams
Missed activity: someone messaged you on Teams
templates.notes: Missed-message notifications are routine and low-suspicion. Universal across Microsoft 365 orgs.

templates.name: OneDrive – Storage Almost Full
suggested_subject_lines: Action needed: free up space or files may stop syncing
You're running out of OneDrive storage
templates.notes: Storage-limit lure pressures users with loss of sync. Pairs with the SharePoint share template.
